harnesses.name: declassification-reason-lint
harnesses.claim: Every Rust CT declassification, high-level CT final-decision, and core fixed-secret export boundary has a reviewable call-site literal and rejects known placeholder wording.

harnesses.name: declassification-reason-lint-fixtures
harnesses.claim: The boundary-reason lint accepts meaningful declassification and export boundaries while rejecting placeholder, generic, dynamic, and macro-generated reasons.

harnesses.name: fail-closed-initialization-lint
harnesses.claim: Production secret-storage source does not discard try_* initialization results through explicit drop, .ok(), or unhandled underscore bindings, or call the lossy pool allocate() shape.

harnesses.name: fail-closed-initialization-lint-fixtures
harnesses.claim: The initialization lint accepts checked propagation and rejects explicit drop, .ok(), unhandled underscore bindings, and lossy pool allocation.

harnesses.name: path-specific-codegen
harnesses.claim: Public secret and CT paths reach the reviewed backend without forbidden substitutions.

harnesses.name: codegen-profile-matrix
harnesses.claim: Path-specific codegen invariants survive opt 2/3/s/z, Thin/Fat LTO, cgu 1/many, and unwind/abort.

harnesses.name: allocation-quarantine
harnesses.claim: Released SecretVec allocations do not retain full secret markers after drop or growth.

harnesses.name: protection-fault-model
harnesses.claim: Required setup failures roll back established model resources and never return live storage.

harnesses.name: concurrency-loom
harnesses.claim: Consume-once, pool reuse, and generation ordering hold under hand-maintained modeled interleavings.

harnesses.name: dependency-policy
harnesses.claim: Every independent Cargo graph uses approved licenses and registries, rejects Git sources and wildcard requirements, and reports duplicate versions.

harnesses.name: bounded-kani
harnesses.claim: Bounded clearing, CT equivalence, capacity, replacement, and protection-report properties hold.

harnesses.name: platform-independent-miri
harnesses.claim: Core all-feature lifecycle unit tests and portable derive and companion storage paths pass Miri without executing native assembly.

harnesses.name: miri-test-backend-gates
harnesses.claim: Miri syscall and CSPRNG simulators remain restricted to core unit tests, while a normal release build with a manually supplied cfg(miri) retains native paths.

harnesses.name: address-sanitizer
harnesses.claim: Native x86_64 Linux tests pass AddressSanitizer where supported.

harnesses.name: thread-sanitizer
harnesses.claim: Native x86_64 Linux tests pass ThreadSanitizer where supported.

harnesses.name: owned-lifecycle-fuzz
harnesses.claim: Owned growth, replacement, serde limits, and enum transitions receive bounded fuzz smoke coverage.

harnesses.name: ct-primitives-fuzz
harnesses.claim: CT equality, ordering, copy, swap, and lookup receive bounded fuzz smoke coverage.

harnesses.name: linux-core-dump-marker
harnesses.claim: A permitted Linux runner can probe whether a locked marker appears in a generated core dump.

harnesses.name: multi-seed-native-leakage
harnesses.claim: Default and strict comparison profiles cover every listed CT primitive with at least three distinct seeds per native evidence target.

harnesses.name: relative-performance-baseline
harnesses.claim: Specialized wipe paths remain distinct from per-element generic clearing and large wipes remain within the reviewed scaling threshold.

harnesses.name: target-evidence-validation
harnesses.claim: Native, compile-only, WASM, timing, and performance artifacts retain exact target classification and fail closed when malformed.

harnesses.name: verification-negative-fixtures
harnesses.claim: Evidence and codegen validators reject seeded malformed inputs.

harnesses.name: downstream-2.0-migration
harnesses.claim: Representative external consumers compile derives, storage contracts, CT APIs, serde, crypto helpers, and companion storage without workspace feature unification.

harnesses.name: migration-inventory
harnesses.claim: Every catalogued removed or behaviorally changed 1.x API maps to a documented 2.0 replacement section.

harnesses.name: cp21-api-candidate
harnesses.claim: The recorded CP-21 source-level candidate remains byte-for-byte historical under a pinned SHA-256.

harnesses.name: cp22-api-freeze
harnesses.claim: The current 2.0 source inventory matches the reviewed tree, intentional 1.2.5 breaks map to migration guidance, and optional scope has an explicit disposition.

harnesses.name: semantic-public-api
harnesses.claim: Rustdoc-derived public API output for every publishable crate matches the current 2.0 candidate when cargo-public-api 0.52.0 is available.

harnesses.name: release-package-archives
harnesses.claim: Every generated crate archive contains the reviewed workspace sources, coordinated release versions, no forbidden repository-only material, and no relative README links to unpackaged repository documentation.
